subject: [PATCH v5 24/36] mm/mempolicy: add in-kernel MPOL_BIND interfaces for drivers/services

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds two new interfaces to the Linux kernel's memory policy (mempolicy) module: mpol_bind_node() and mpol_private_bind(). These interfaces allow drivers or services to create a mempolicy that is pinned to a specific NUMA node, either publicly (mpol_bind_node()) or privately (mpol_private_bind()). The patch also updates the mpol_set_nodemask function to handle private nodes correctly. This change enables drivers to manage memory policies for VMAs they control, and provides a way for them to bypass certain security checks.
